tidyfault 0.0.0.9
  • Reference

    quantify_binary() Function

    quantify_binary.Rd

    Evaluates whether the top event occurs (system fails) when given whether each basic event occurs or not. Inputs are binary: each event is 0 (did not occur) or 1 (occurred). No probabilities—deterministic evaluation only.

    quantify_binary(f, newdata)

    Arguments

    f

    (Required) Function from formulate(), with one argument per basic event. Used to identify event names and to evaluate the fault tree.

    newdata

    (Required) Binary event states (0/1 or TRUE/FALSE) for each basic event. Either a tibble/data frame with one column per basic event (names from formalArgs(f)), or a single vector/list. A single scenario can be an unnamed vector in formalArgs(f) order (e.g. c(T, T, T, F) or c(1, 1, 0, 1)), or a named vector/list. Values are logical (TRUE/FALSE) or numeric (0/1), where 1/TRUE means the event occurred and 0/FALSE means it did not.

    Value

    A logical vector of outcomes (TRUE = system failure, FALSE = no failure). If newdata is a data frame with multiple rows, returns a vector with one outcome per row. If newdata is a single scenario (vector/list), returns a single logical value.

    Details

    No probabilities are used; the function evaluates the fault tree on the given event states. Outcome is TRUE when f(...) >= 1 (system failure). Logical and 0/1 inputs are coerced to 0/1 internally to match calculate() behavior. When newdata is a data frame, evaluation is vectorized: f is called once with vector columns (one element per row), so many scenarios are handled efficiently. Returns only the outcome vector, similar to R's predict() function style.

    See also

    formulate for creating the function, calculate for the full truth table, quantify_prob for top event failure probability given basic event probabilities, quantify for the unified wrapper function.

    Examples

    library(tidyverse)
library(tidyfault)
library(QCA)
data("fakenodes")
data("fakeedges")

# Pipe-friendly: single scenario in event order (e.g. A, B, C, D)
curate(nodes = fakenodes, edges = fakeedges) %>%
  equate() %>%
  formulate() %>%
  quantify_binary(c(T, T, T, F))
#> [1] TRUE

f <- curate(nodes = fakenodes, edges = fakeedges) %>%
  equate() %>%
  formulate()

# Single scenario: unnamed vector in event order
f %>% quantify_binary(c(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE, FALSE))
#> [1] FALSE

# Single scenario: named vector (logical or 0/1)
one_scenario <- setNames(c(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE, FALSE), formalArgs(f))
f %>% quantify_binary(one_scenario)
#> [1] FALSE

# Tibble of scenarios: returns vector of outcomes (vectorized, one call to f for all rows)
scenarios_tbl <- tibble(
  A = c(1L, 0L, 1L),
  B = c(0L, 1L, 1L),
  C = c(1L, 0L, 0L),
  D = c(0L, 1L, 1L)
)
f %>% quantify_binary(scenarios_tbl)  # Returns logical vector
#> [1] FALSE FALSE  TRUE

    Contents
